customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety overall — Leland is a warm, engaged, long-tenured solar prospect with genuine pre-existing interest. His primary live concern is an unresolved Xcel billing dispute that produced a $1,039 back-charge he discovered during this call. He is resilient about it (self-contextualized the anomaly) but the dispute is not settled and may affect his perception of his actual usage baseline.
unresolved_concerns: Xcel billing dispute: Leland's bill history is partially unreliable due to a meter/address mix-up. The last 3-4 months are accurate; prior months (including last summer's peak) were billed to a neighbor. The $1,039 back-charge was discovered live on this call and has not been resolved with Xcel.
Bill photo not confirmed received: Parker asked Leland to text a photo of the bill but the call ended before receipt was confirmed. The design may not have the full usage history.
Wife on title not included in appointment: Leland confirmed his wife is on the title. She was not asked about availability and is not confirmed as a participant in the closer call.
sensitive_topics_raised: Live financial surprise: $1,039 Xcel back-charge discovered during the call — Leland processed it calmly but it is a real financial stress point.
Billing history unreliability: Leland's usage data for periods prior to the last 3-4 months is compromised by the meter error. The design should be based only on the accurate recent months.
exact_promises_rep_made: Closer will call Leland tomorrow (the day after this call) at 9AM.
Closer will email the solar design during the call so Leland can view it on his computer.
Parker will send a text with the date and time (tomorrow at 9AM) after the call.
Parker told Leland to text him with any questions and he will respond as fast as he can.
closer_should_know: Wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment — confirm at the top of the call whether she is present or available, and if not, reschedule to include her before proceeding with the proposal. The Xcel billing dispute ($1,039 back-charge, meter/address mix-up) is unresolved; Leland's usage history is only reliable for the last 3-4 months. Acknowledge the billing situation briefly before presenting the design — Leland is resilient about it but it was a live financial surprise on the setter call and a one-sentence acknowledgment will rebuild the trust gap the setter left open.
